Reasoning
Q1
Directions:Read the information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
At a reunion party, eight friends named P, Q, R,
S, T, U, V and W sit together on a round table
facing the center. Interestingly, all eight friends
are pursuing bachelor’s degrees in different
subjects namely Mathematics, English, Business
studies, Polity, Physics, Chemistry, Mythology
and Hindi. They are not necessarily seated in
the mentioned order. R sits adjacent to S who is
studying Business studies. Q is studying Polity
and does not sit adjacent to R or S. U is sitting
fifth to the left of P and is studying Physics. One
who is studying Chemistry sits immediate left to
U. V sits opposite to P. Neither W nor T is
immediate neighbor of V. One who is studying
Hindi sits fifth to the left of V, who is learning
Mythology. T is studying English and sits next to
the one who is studying Hindi.
What does the person study who is sitting third
to the left of R?
(A) Mathematics
(B) Physics
(C) Mythology
(D) Polity
(E) Hindi

Q6
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below:
Seven persons i.e. Z, Y, X, W, V, U and T of
different ages i.e. 6, 15, 19, 23, 30, 45, 60 years
are sitting in a circular table facing towards the
center. Note- No two consecutive letters are in
alphabetical order. Person’s age must not be a
factor or a multiple of the sum of the ages of
their immediate neighbors. X sits immediate to
the right of the person whose age is 15. One
person sits between V and X . One person sits
between Z and the person whose age is 6 . Age
of V is twice the age of the person who sits 2nd
to the right of V. Di erence between the age of
the immediate neighbors of T is more than 14.
Age of U must not be multiple of 10. Sum of the
ages of immediate neighbors of X is 34. Y is
younger than Z.
What is the age of Z?
(A) 15
(B) 60
(C) 23
(D) 6
(E) 19

Q25
Text Solution:
They had candies with them which are
consecutive multiples of 2 starting from R
clockwise. R had 8 candies. Hence, the number

of candies previously was 8, 10, 12, 14, ... After
shuffling, a person will have (n + m)/2 candies,
where n = no. of candies he had and m = no. of
candies he received.
A and E are the only persons at the table whose
names start with vowels and hence facing
inward.
Also, most of the persons (except A and E) are
facing outward and will give candies to the one
sitting second to his right.
C has 34 candies. Three persons are sitting in
between E and C. E is an immediate neighbour
of R.
One person is sitting in between C and L. A is
sitting second to the left of L. So, cases 1(i) and
1(ii) are invalid and also, L will not get A's candies
as A is facing inward. As C will get L's candies,
so for C, (m +n)/2 = 34 and n = m + 4. From this,
we get, m = 32, n = 36. Hence, L will now have
32/2 = 16 candies only (half of his candies as he
won't get candies from anyone else). Hence,
original no. of candies with A = L - 4 = 32 - 4 =
28. Now, A must-have 28/2 + (28 - 4)/2 = 26
candies.
Now, R has 4 of his candies + m candies.
Clockwise from R, for each person, n/2 = 5, 6, 7,
... Y is sitting fifth to the left of E. In case 2(i), the
person sitting second to the right of R will have
n/2 = 6, m/2 = 4, i.e., a total of 10 candies. The
